Architecture: simulator ground truth, two-speed rendering

A mission-day simulator is the sole ground truth: it runs forward cheaply and adjudicates any state on demand through executable doctrine, so correct calls are computed for never-seen states. Trajectories are sampled, never enumerated; training data is inexhaustible.

Two-speed rendering keeps the LLM out of the inner loop: the SIM emits a typed canonical log (the episode graph), and an offline LLM-built compositional surface bank renders it to language on CPU, fast enough that one core outruns the training GPU. Input surfaces vary through fragment banks, a bounded full-paraphrase fraction, and bank refresh between epochs; output commands have no variation at all, canonical and schema-parsed. Read many, write canonical.

This extends program-author-LLM-stylist (node 38) from documents to a running world. The predefinition worry dissolves because the state space is sampled, not mapped: the SIM needs only run-forward and adjudicate-on-demand, and every epoch can see fresh trajectories. Measured feasibility from prior runs: training consumes about 30k tokens per second (about 60 documents per second) on one L40S at mini scale; per-document LLM rendering manages about 25 documents per second per GPU, while bank rendering reaches 100-300 documents per second per CPU core, with the bank build itself one to two GPU-hours amortized across all future corpora, so simulator-fed training is cheaper than the finite-corpus pipeline it replaces. The interaction ladder is graded: fresh-epoch corpora first (regenerate between rungs and epochs, training loop untouched), interactive evaluation immediately (the SIM adjudicates the model's calls; counterfactual re-rolls make surface memorization score zero), true streaming only if fresh epochs show staleness, and expert iteration as the eventual training-with-the-simulator form, with reinforcement learning explicitly off the table. The output side answers the command-mapping question: SGM outputs are typed canonical forms parsed programmatically, so no LLM sits between the model and the SIM. The bank-collapse risk (the model learning bank surfaces as a codebook) is guarded by compositional slots, the paraphrase fraction, refresh, the tier-1 language mass, and an n-gram collapse gauge, with the counterfactual battery as the backstop.
